codeReview.summary: MCP client implementation is functionally complete and correct. Implements JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio transport, protocol handshake, tool discovery (tools/list), and tool execution (tools/call). McpToolWrapper properly implements the Tool trait for ToolRegistry integration. McpManager provides deferred/lazy loading support. All verification steps pass (cargo build --workspace, cargo test -p swell-tools -- mcp with 5 tests, cargo clippy with no warnings).

issues.description: In call_tool(), the arguments parsing fallback could silently pass raw strings to the MCP server if JSON parsing fails: serde_json::from_str(args_str).unwrap_or(arguments). This is defensive but could cause unexpected behavior. Consider returning an error instead of falling back.
McpToolWrapper lacks #[derive(Debug)] since it contains McpClient which is Debug. This makes debugging harder but is not blocking.

list_tools_deferred() drops the read lock before calling list_tools() in the empty case, allowing a potential brief window for another task to populate tools. This results in at most one redundant fetch; correctness is maintained.

McpClient is Clone and shares underlying process via Arc<RwLock<Option<McpProcess>>>. Cloning creates shared state - if one clone calls disconnect(), all clones become disconnected. This follows Rust reference semantics but could surprise users expecting independent clones.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The transcript skeleton shows the worker used Skill 'rust-worker' but this skill file doesn't exist in .factory/skills/rust-worker/. The AGENTS.md documents skills in .factory/skills/ as the canonical location. The handoff indicates followedProcedure=true, so the skill was presumably loaded from elsewhere in the system.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton shows Skill with 'rust-worker'. File /Users/ludwigjonsson/.factory/skills/rust-worker/SKILL.md does not exist.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The MCP protocol initialization sequence (initialize → response → notifications/initialized) is implemented correctly but is a subtle protocol detail. No comment in the code explains WHY this sequence is required. Future developers might inadvertently 'simplify' this.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: mcp.rs:initialize_protocol() sends initialize, reads response, then sends notifications/initialized. MCP spec requires this exact sequence.

summary: PASS - Feature 'tools-mcp-client' successfully implements MCP client for external tool servers with JSON-RPC over stdio transport, tool discovery via tools/list, tool execution via tools/call, McpToolWrapper integrating with ToolRegistry, and McpManager with deferred loading support. All verification steps pass (cargo build, cargo test with 5 MCP tests, cargo clippy). No blocking issues identified.
